title: ampelocissus pentaphylla
intro: Ampelocissus pentaphylla, also known as the five-leaved grape vine, is a tropical plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Ampelocissus pentaphylla are believed to include the relief of fever, diarrhea, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to protect against cancer and cardiovascular disease. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying and powdering the leaves or roots, which can then be used in teas, capsules, or other medicinal formulations.
